creator: ariff@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Tue, 20 Nov 2007 15:02:03 +0100
Ed Schouten <ed@fxq.nl> wrote:
> * Ariff Abdullah <ariff@FreeBSD.org> wrote:
> > On Tue, 20 Nov 2007 09:55:25 +0100
> > Ed Schouten <ed@fxq.nl> wrote:
> > > * Ariff Abdullah <ariff@FreeBSD.org> wrote:
> > > > Try various config options through hint.pcm.%d.config ,
> > > > possibly gpio0,1,2 , ovref, etc..
> > > 
> > > I can confirm that setting hint.pcm.0.config to gpio0 the audio
> > > output works, but the headjack doesn't, which means rerouting to
> > > the headjack is done in software. I'm not an expert when it
> > > comes to sound cards. Is this normal? If so, does the snd_hda
> > > driver support?
> > > 
> > 
> > Have you tried ovrefXX ? Possibly ovref80, etc. Multiples options
> > can be combined, ssv or csv.
> 
> ovref and ovref80 have no effect, but if I use ovref50 or ovref100,
> audio output also works on the headjack, but it doesn't silence
> output on the internal speakers.
>

Try http://people.freebsd.org/~ariff/test/hdac.c . Unset all quirky
.config first.


--
Ariff Abdullah
FreeBSD

... Recording in stereo is obviously too advanced
    and confusing for us idiot ***** users :P ........
